Home Blog

Internal Linking Best Practices for SEO

Internal linking is one of the most underutilized SEO techniques. When done correctly, internal links help search engines understand your site structure, distribute page authority, and improve user navigation throughout your website.

Why Internal Links Matter

Internal links serve multiple purposes in your SEO strategy. They help search engine crawlers discover new pages, establish information hierarchy, and spread link equity across your site. A strong internal linking strategy can improve your overall search engine rankings significantly.

Choosing the Right Anchor Text

Anchor text is the clickable text in a hyperlink. For internal links, use descriptive anchor text that accurately represents the linked page content. Avoid generic phrases like click here or read more. Instead, use keyword-rich anchor text that provides context about the destination page.

Link to Relevant Content

Only create internal links between pages that are topically related. Linking your keyword research guide to your content about on-page optimization makes sense because both topics fall under the SEO umbrella. Random or forced links can confuse both users and search engines.

Maintain a Reasonable Link Count

While there is no strict limit on internal links per page, adding too many can dilute link equity and overwhelm readers. Aim for three to five contextual internal links per thousand words of content. Focus on quality over quantity for the best results.

Fix Broken Internal Links

Regularly audit your internal links to identify and fix broken links. Broken links waste crawl budget and create poor user experiences. Tools like Screaming Frog and Google Search Console can help you find and resolve broken internal links efficiently.
